title: Bedding Test
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"91m0d","text":"Used to assess nigrostriatal sensorimotor function in mice. Based on the Deacon 2006 protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Provide few pellet of food (Standard diet, SDS, R1/CRM/R3) (around 2-3 pellets for a total of 5g) as well as water ad libitum.
Transfer the mice as single cage into a “Green line” cage (Techniplast, USA).
Place a 5 x 5 cm cellulose paper tissue folded in the right corner of the cage.
Place the cage in a ventilated rack with temperature, humidity and light control for 48h.
At the end of the 48h check the animal cage and take a picture.

Score the bedding as follow: 0. The mice did not touch the paper square. The mice unfolded the paper square but did not tear it up. The mice unfolded and started to tore up <10% of the paper square. The mice tore up the paper square <50% The mice tore up the paper square, but the bedding is not perfect. Perfect bedding
creator: Jacquelyn Haytayan
